create_circle.name: create_circle
create_circle.description: Creates a CircleGameObject that takes in circle shape properties.

create_rectangle.name: create_rectangle
create_rectangle.description: Creates a RectangleGameObject that takes in rectangle shape properties.

create_triangle.name: create_triangle
create_triangle.description: Creates a TriangleGameObject that takes in an downright isosceles triangle shape properties.

create_sprite.name: create_sprite
create_sprite.description: Creates a GameObject that contains a Sprite image reference. Source Academy assets can be used by specifying path without the prepend. Source Academy assets can be found at https://source-academy-assets.s3-ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/ with Ctrl+f '.png'. Phaser assets can be found at https://labs.phaser.io/assets/. If Phaser assets are unavailable, go to https://github.com/photonstorm/phaser3-examples/tree/master/public/assets to get the asset path and append it to https://labs.phaser.io/assets/. Assets from other websites can also be used if they support Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS), but the full path must be specified.

create_text.name: create_text
create_text.description: Creates a GameObject that contains a text reference.

query_color.name: query_color
query_color.description: Queries the [r, g, b, a] color property of the GameObject.

query_flip.name: query_flip
query_flip.description: Queries the [x, y] flip property of the GameObject.

query_id.name: query_id
query_id.description: Queries the id of the GameObject. The id of a GameObject is in the order of creation, starting from 0.

query_position.name: query_position
query_position.description: Queries the [x, y] position transform of the GameObject.

query_rotation.name: query_rotation
query_rotation.description: Queries the z-rotation transform of the GameObject.

query_scale.name: query_scale
query_scale.description: Queries the [x, y] scale transform of the GameObject.

query_text.name: query_text
query_text.description: Queries the text of a Text GameObject.

update_color.name: update_color
update_color.description: Updates the color of the GameObject. Note that the value is modulo 256, so passing values greater than 255 is allowed.

update_flip.name: update_flip
update_flip.description: Updates the flip state of the GameObject.

update_position.name: update_position
update_position.description: Updates the position transform of the GameObject.

update_rotation.name: update_rotation
update_rotation.description: Updates the rotation transform of the GameObject.

update_scale.name: update_scale
update_scale.description: Updates the scale transform of the GameObject.

update_to_top.name: update_to_top
update_to_top.description: Renders this GameObject in front of all other GameObjects.

set_fps.name: set_fps
set_fps.description: Sets the frames per second of the canvas, which should be between the MIN_FPS and MAX_FPS. It ranges between 1 and 120, with the default target as 30. This function should not be called in the update function.

set_dimensions.name: set_dimensions
set_dimensions.description: Sets the dimensions of the canvas, which should be between the min and max widths and height.

set_scale.name: set_scale
set_scale.description: Sets the scale (zoom) of the pixels in the canvas. If scale is doubled, then the number of units across would be halved. This has a side effect of making the game pixelated if scale > 1. The default scale is 1.

get_game_time.name: get_game_time
get_game_time.description: Gets the current in-game time, which is based off the start time. This function should be called in your update function.

get_loop_count.name: get_loop_count
get_loop_count.description: Gets the current loop count, which is the number of frames that have run. Depends on the framerate set for how fast this changes. This function should be called in your update function.

enable_debug.name: enable_debug
enable_debug.description: Enables debug mode. Hit box interaction between pointer and GameObjects are shown with a green outline in debug mode. Hit box interaction between GameObjects is based off a rectangular area instead, which is not reflected. debug_log(...) information is shown on the top-left corner of the canvas.

debug_log.name: debug_log
debug_log.description: Logs any information passed into it within the update_loop. Displays the information in the top-left corner of the canvas only if debug mode is enabled. Calling display within the update_loop function will not work as intended, so use debug_log instead.

input_key_down.name: input_key_down
input_key_down.description: Detects if a key input is pressed down. This function must be called in your update function to detect inputs. To get specific keys, go to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent/key#result.

input_left_mouse_down.name: input_left_mouse_down
input_left_mouse_down.description: Detects if the left mouse button is pressed down. This function should be called in your update function.

input_right_mouse_down.name: input_right_mouse_down
input_right_mouse_down.description: Detects if the right mouse button is pressed down. This function should be called in your update function.

pointer_over_gameobject.name: pointer_over_gameobject
pointer_over_gameobject.description: Detects if the (mouse) pointer is over the gameobject. This function should be called in your update function.

gameobjects_overlap.name: gameobjects_overlap
gameobjects_overlap.description: Checks if two gameobjects overlap with each other, using a rectangular bounding box. This bounding box is rectangular, for all GameObjects. This function should be called in your update function.

update_loop.name: update_loop
update_loop.description: This sets the update loop in the canvas. The update loop is run once per frame, so it depends on the fps set for the number of times this loop is run. There should only be one update_loop called. All game logic should be handled within your update_function. You cannot create GameObjects inside the update_loop. game_state is an array that can be modified to store anything.

build_game.name: build_game
build_game.description: Builds the game. Processes the initialization and updating of the game. All created GameObjects and their properties are passed into the game.

create_audio.name: create_audio
create_audio.description: Create an audio clip that can be referenced. Source Academy assets can be found at https://source-academy-assets.s3-ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/ with Ctrl+f '.mp3'. Phaser audio assets can be found at https://labs.phaser.io/assets/audio. Phaser sound effects assets can be found at https://labs.phaser.io/assets/audio/SoundEffects/. If Phaser assets are unavailable, go to https://github.com/photonstorm/phaser3-examples/tree/master/public/assets to get the asset path and append it to https://labs.phaser.io/assets/. This function should not be called in your update function.

loop_audio.name: loop_audio
loop_audio.description: Loops the audio clip provided, which will play the audio clip indefinitely. Setting whether an audio clip should loop be done outside the update function.

play_audio.name: play_audio
play_audio.description: Plays the audio clip, and stops when the audio clip is over.
